message:
fix(storage): apply busy_timeout to primary writer connection + migrate_database_to_version

Fourth-instance follow-up to f1ce5db4. Two more Class-4 SQLITE_BUSY
hazards uncovered while sweeping for missed sites:

StorageHandle::with_config (the primary writer connection):
  SCHEMA_SQL embeds journal_mode/foreign_keys/synchronous PRAGMAs but
  no busy_timeout, and `initialize_schema` short-circuits on reopen of
  an up-to-date DB without re-running SCHEMA_SQL — so the writer was
  silently running with default (zero) busy_timeout. Any
  spawn_blocking_storage reader (now opened via open_read_storage_conn)
  would race the writer's WAL-recovery / schema-init / ALTER PRAGMAs
  and abort with SQLITE_BUSY.

migrate_database_to_version:
  Same class. Migrations issue ALTER/CREATE statements requiring the
  write lock; without busy_timeout an active reader makes the
  migration abort at the first ALTER.
